title: Thomassen's Polynomial-time Colorability Program
statement_text: For fixed integers $k$ and $q$, can the $k$-colorability of graphs of girth $q$ embedded in a fixed surface be tested in polynomial time?
context_text: Thomassen [17] initiated a research program posing this question. The current paper series addresses the triangle-free ($q \geq 4$, $k = 3$) case; Theorem 1.3 is described as completing one of two missing steps toward a polynomial-time algorithm for 3-colorability of triangle-free graphs on a fixed surface.
paper_contribution: Proves Theorem 1.3—bounding the sum of excess face sizes of 4-critical triangle-free graphs embedded in surfaces in terms of genus, triangle count, and non-facial 4-cycles—which is the cornerstone ingredient enabling a polynomial-time 3-colorability algorithm for triangle-free graphs on a fixed surface, thereby completing one of the two missing steps in Thomassen's program.
notes: Statement reconstructed from prose description in the introduction; the bibliography entry for [17] is not present in the extracted text so the year cannot be confirmed.
paper_title: Three-coloring triangle-free graphs on surfaces IV. Bounding face sizes of 4-critical graphs

title: 4-colorability on Fixed Surfaces (Open Step in Thomassen's Program)
statement_text: Whether 4-colorability of graphs embedded in a fixed surface can be tested in polynomial time remains open; the authors remark that prospects for its resolution in the near future are not very bright.
context_text: After announcing that Theorem 1.3 completes one of two missing steps in Thomassen's polynomial-time colorability program, the authors identify the remaining open step as the analogous question for 4-coloring graphs on a fixed surface.
notes: Stated informally in the introduction as the outstanding second step of Thomassen's research program; no formal problem number is assigned.
paper_title: Three-coloring triangle-free graphs on surfaces IV. Bounding face sizes of 4-critical graphs
